title: Crawl Graphs, Frontier Strategy, Visited State, Depth, and Determinism · Language Clinic: Crawl Graph Model
summary: URLs are marked visited after download, duplicates race into the queue, depth changes with concurrency, and restart cannot explain which parent discovered a page. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawl graph model increment in deterministic crawl-frontier state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.
Retain parent page, node or metadata source, raw reference, resolved URL, relation, anchor context, decision, and rejection reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-crawl-graph-model — Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-graph-frontier-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-graph-frontier-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_crawl_graph_frontier_3(admitted_urls: int = 64, reserved_urls: int = 64, completed_urls: int = 58, rejected_urls: int = 6, duplicate_schedules: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if admitted_urls < 1 or reserved_urls < 0:
        return False, reserved_urls, "frontier-count"
    if reserved_urls != admitted_urls:
        return False, reserved_urls, "reservation-gap"
    if completed_urls + rejected_urls != reserved_urls:
        return False, completed_urls, "state-reconciliation"
    if duplicate_schedules > 0:
        return False, duplicate_schedules, "duplicate-schedule"
    assert completed_urls + rejected_urls == reserved_urls and duplicate_schedules == 0
    return True, completed_urls, "deterministic-crawl-frontier"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-crawl-graph-model-14zvwv
# Competency: scraper-crawl-graph-model.
# Case 8057: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_crawl_graph_model_14zvwv(admitted_urls: int = 64, reserved_urls: int = 64, completed_urls: int = 58, rejected_urls: int = 6, duplicate_schedules: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_14zvwv = "14zvwv-vwvz41-8057"
    assert evidence_variant_14zvwv
    evidence_axes_14zvwv = (
        "authorization14zvwv",
        "url8057",
        "responsevwvz41",
        "tree14zvwv8057",
        "frontier805714zvwv",
        "reportvwvz418057",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_14zvwv)) == 6
    if admitted_urls < 1 or reserved_urls < 0:
        return False, reserved_urls, "frontier-count"
    if reserved_urls != admitted_urls:
        return False, reserved_urls, "reservation-gap"
    if completed_urls + rejected_urls != reserved_urls:
        return False, completed_urls, "state-reconciliation"
    if duplicate_schedules > 0:
        return False, duplicate_schedules, "duplicate-schedule"
    assert completed_urls + rejected_urls == reserved_urls and duplicate_schedules == 0
    return True, completed_urls, "deterministic-crawl-frontier"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Crawl Graph Model structural check is only one gate. deterministic crawl-frontier state machine must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Build Crawl Graph Model evidence · Theory Crawl Graph Model, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Crawl Graph Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Build Crawl Graph Model evidence · Theory Crawl Graph Model, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 1218 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile crawl graph, atomic scheduling, frontier state, parents, depth, and deterministic order; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.

For Authorized crawling and Python Build Crawl Graph Model evidence · Theory Crawl Graph Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: A flat visited set captures the complete crawl graph.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Crawl Graph Model model · Theory Crawl Graph Model
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Crawl Graph Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 8599 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile crawl graph, atomic scheduling, frontier state, parents, depth, and deterministic order; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.

steps.why: Retrieving Crawl Graph Model through the concrete deterministic crawl-frontier state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Crawl Graph Model model · Theory Crawl Graph Model, keep Crawl Graph Model connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: scraper-crawl-graph-model-14zvwv.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-crawl-graph-model-14zvwv(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_crawl_graph_model_14zvwv\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted_urls < 1 or reserved_urls < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reserved_urls != admitted_urls)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed_urls \+ rejected_urls != reserved_urls)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?duplicate_schedules > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
